sn47.14:1.1: This must have taken place during the Buddha’s final journey depicted in the Mahāparinibbānasutta, immediately after crossing the Ganges at Pāṭaligāma (DN 16:2.1.1). It is, however, not mentioned there. Moggallāna’s death is not depicted in any early texts, as a result of which it became elaborated in a popular legend of later times.
sn47.14:2.3: The text uses the same present tense as the previous sentence, but it must refer to the past. Bodhi discusses variants here.
sn47.14:2.8: Garubhāvanīyā is a dvanda compound, “respected and admired” (see AN 5.81:1.1). | The four assemblies are monks, nuns, laymen, and laywomen. The number four indicates inclusivity.
